DIALOGS.DISCLAIMER_TEXT_1: The training plans, recommendations, and information provided by PACR are for general informational purposes only. They do not constitute medical advice and are not a substitute for professional medical consultation, diagnosis, or treatment.
DIALOGS.DISCLAIMER_TEXT_2: Use at your own risk. You are solely responsible for your training decisions and physical activities. PACR and its operators assume no liability for injuries, health issues, or any other damages that may result from following training plans or recommendations provided through this application.
DIALOGS.DISCLAIMER_TEXT_3: We strongly recommend consulting a qualified physician before starting any new exercise program, especially if you have pre-existing health conditions.
DIALOGS.DISCLAIMER_TEXT_4: Data imported from FIT files is processed for display purposes only and does not represent a medical evaluation. Any metrics shown (heart rate, pace, power, etc.) should not be used for medical decision-making.
DIALOGS.FEEDBACK_THANKS: Thank you! Your feedback has been sent.
DIALOGS.FEEDBACK_FILL_FIELDS: Please fill in both subject and message.
DIALOGS.FEEDBACK_ERROR: Failed to send feedback. Please try again.
DIALOGS.ACWR_DESCRIPTION: The ACWR (Acute:Chronic Workload Ratio) compares your short-term training load (last 7 days) with the long-term (last 28 days). It shows whether you're increasing your training safely or overloading.
DIALOGS.ACWR_BUILDING_DESC: Underloading — you're training significantly less than usual. Your training volume can be increased.
DIALOGS.ACWR_OPTIMAL_DESC: Sweet spot — good balance between load and recovery. Your injury risk is low.
DIALOGS.ACWR_CAUTION_DESC: Increased injury risk — you're increasing training faster than recommended. Stabilize your volume.
DIALOGS.ACWR_OVERLOAD_DESC: High injury risk — your body is significantly overloaded. Reduce training volume.
DIALOGS.ACWR_TIP: Ideally increase your training volume by a maximum of 10% per week to stay in the optimal range.
DIALOGS.STRAIN_DESCRIPTION: The 21-day Training Load (Strain) measures the cumulative load of your last 21 training days. It considers duration, distance, and heart rate of your sessions and gives you an overview of how much your body is currently being stressed.
DIALOGS.STRAIN_LOW_DESC: Little training stimulus — ideal for recovery phases or getting back after a break. Not enough for long-term performance gains.
DIALOGS.STRAIN_MODERATE_DESC: Solid training foundation — you're building fitness consistently without overloading. Good balance between load and recovery.
DIALOGS.STRAIN_HIGH_DESC: Intensive training phase — your body is being heavily challenged. Pay special attention to sufficient sleep and recovery.
DIALOGS.STRAIN_VERY_HIGH_DESC: High load level — risk of overtraining and injuries increases. Plan deliberate rest days and reduce intensity.
DIALOGS.STRAIN_TIP: Training load is individual — compare mainly with your own values over time, not with others.
DIALOGS.READINESS_TITLE: What does the Readiness Score mean?
DIALOGS.READINESS_DESCRIPTION: The Readiness Score shows on a scale of 0 to 100 how ready your body is for the next training session. It considers your current training load, recovery time, and load trends from recent days.
DIALOGS.READINESS_EXCELLENT_DESC: Your body is fully recovered and performance-ready — ideal time for intense sessions, intervals, or races.
DIALOGS.READINESS_GOOD_DESC: Good training readiness — you can train normally and handle moderate to high loads well.
DIALOGS.READINESS_MODERATE_DESC: Slight fatigue noticeable — adjust intensity and opt for easy runs or active recovery.
DIALOGS.READINESS_LOW_DESC: Significant fatigue — your body needs recovery. Prioritize rest days, sleep, and regeneration to avoid overtraining.
DIALOGS.READINESS_TIP: The Readiness Score is a guideline — always listen to your body and adjust your training accordingly.
DIALOGS.PR_DETAIL_FORMAT_ERROR: Invalid format. Please use MM:SS or H:MM:SS.
DIALOGS.PACE_DESCRIPTION: Enter any two values to calculate your performance metrics. Values update live as you type.
DIALOGS.PRO_UNLOCK: Upgrade to PACR Pro to unlock this feature.

BODY_DATA.TITLE: Body
BODY_DATA.OBSERVATIONS_PLACEHOLDER: How did you feel this morning? Any dietary changes or recent heavy training sessions...
BODY_DATA.BP_FORMAT_ERROR: Blood pressure must use the format systolic/diastolic, e.g. 120/80.
BODY_DATA.MIN_ONE_METRIC: Enter at least one metric before saving.
BODY_DATA.SAVE_ERROR: Failed to save. Please try again.
BODY_DATA.CYCLE_SUBTITLE: Syncing your biology with your training.
BODY_DATA.CYCLE_START_Q: Did your new cycle start today?
BODY_DATA.PHASE_FOLLICULAR_PERFORMANCE: High energy levels. Estrogen is rising, improving muscle strength and recovery speed. Great window for PR attempts or speedwork.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_FOLLICULAR_FUELING: Higher carbohydrate tolerance. Focus on complex carbs for sustained intensity.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_OVULATION_PERFORMANCE: Peak strength and coordination. Testosterone peaks alongside estrogen — ideal for power training and race efforts.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_OVULATION_FUELING: Moderate carbs and protein. Hydration is key as body temperature rises slightly.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_LUTEAL_PERFORMANCE: Energy may dip in late luteal phase. Progesterone rises, increasing perceived effort. Favour steady-state and tempo runs.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_LUTEAL_FUELING: Increased caloric needs. Focus on iron-rich foods and complex carbs to support elevated metabolism.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_MENSTRUAL_PERFORMANCE: Rest and recovery priority. Low hormone levels — listen to your body. Light movement supports wellbeing.
BODY_DATA.PHASE_MENSTRUAL_FUELING: Iron and magnesium intake is important. Anti-inflammatory foods help manage discomfort.
BODY_DATA.NO_ENTRIES: No entries yet. Start logging your symptoms.
BODY_DATA.NOTES_FEELING: How are you feeling overall? Any specific pains or breakthroughs?
BODY_DATA.INSPIRATION_QUOTE: Strength is built in the moments you want to stop.
